proposed.claim: Skills coordinate authoring, query, and presentation: `/assert` adds beliefs from artifacts, entities, or reasoning; `/assertions` queries and traverses the graph; `/materialize` turns directives into concrete tasks; `/present-codepath` walks a codepath - a code-anchored collection rendered as a narrated, branching tour of real source that can also run as a test suite (`mix cb.verify.codepath`).

evidence.detail: Supersedes a480 to retire /assert-session from the framework skill set. Audit found it an un-ported origin-system extract: its error taxonomy (a050-a054) dangles in cb:, its policy-kind-on-primitives instruction violates c057, and its write step bypasses the preflight/adjudicate/import flow. Its unique content - conversation-parse heuristics for user-stated rules and agent reasoning errors - folded into the operator's /end sweep (Step 2), scoped to the operator ecosystem rather than framework-shipped. The rot pattern matched a386: every broken part cached canon as prose; the surviving parts were judgment content with no graph counterpart.

reasoning: Operator-directed fold (2026-06-11): /assert-session retired from the framework skill set after audit found it un-ported origin canon (dangling a050-a054 taxonomy, c057 violation, write-flow bypass). Its unique extraction heuristics moved to the operator /end sweep; the framework skill list shrinks by one. Successor carries the four remaining skills verbatim.
